name: Shanghai Fish Soup
recipeIngredient: 8 oz Firm tofu, drained and cut into 1/2 inch cubes
1 large Green onion, cut diagonally into 1-inch pieces
recipeInstructions:
1. Soak mushrooms in warm water until caps are tender, 15 to 30 minutes. Drain and squeeze gently to remove some of the water. Cut off and discard stems; thinly slice caps. Set aside.
2. Rinse fish and pat dry. Cut fillets into 1-by 1 1/2 inch pieces. Set aside.
3. Place a wok or 3 to 4 quart saucepan over high heat. When hot, add oil and swirl to coat cooking surface.
4. Add ginger and stir until fragrant, about 30 seconds.
5. Add mushrooms, fish, and red-in-snow; stir until fish turns opaque on the outside, about 1 minute.
6. Stir in broth, tofu, soy sauce, sherry, and sugar; bring soup to a boil, stirring occasionally.
7. Pour into a tureen and sprinkle with green onions and sesame oil. Makes 4 to 6 servings.
8. Per serving: 201 Calories, 20 g Protein, 7 g Carbohydrates, 2 g Saturated Fat, 3 g Monounsaturated Fat, 4 g Polyunsaturated Fat, .2 Omega-3 Fat, 33 mg Cholesterol, 970 mg Sodium.
9. Note: Fish, Cod, Rockfish, Snapper, Orange Roughy can be substituted for catfish.
